steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states 'The Crown of Columbus' is set largely on a Caribbean island, outside Ojibwe communities. The correct answer must be directly supported by — and only by — what the passage says.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It has been adapted into a movie.: adds information not stated in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — It isn't set in a rural Ojibwe community.: states the novel is not set in a rural Ojibwe community — directly supported by the passage's description of a Caribbean island setting. ✓
  • Choice C — It contains very little dialogue.: introduces a claim not supported by the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — Its main characters are Ojibwe.: asserts something the passage does not establish. ✗
That is the correct answer.
